L1.area: Enterprise security and authentication provider integration

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where the server fails to verify user credentials against the directory service when a specific transport security mechanism is enabled. An attacker can log in with any valid username and an arbitrary password.

L3.area: LDAP authentication realm and its TLS connection handling logic
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in the LDAP authentication provider where, when configured to use STARTTLS for securing the connection, user credentials are set in the directory context but never actually verified against the LDAP server. An attacker can log in with any valid username and an arbitrary password.

ground_truth.reason: When LDAP is configured with STARTTLS, the authentication method sets credentials in the LDAP context environment but never triggers an actual verification against the LDAP server, allowing login with any valid username and an arbitrary password.
